title: Paspalum langei
intro: Paspalum langei is a perennial grass species native to South America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat digestive issues, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum langei include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Paspalum langei is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Paspalum langei is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae. The genus Paspalum is a group of grasses commonly known as paspalums. Paspalum langei is a part of the tribe Paniceae, which includes various types of grasses. The classification of Paspalum langei is further detailed at the species level, but the above-mentioned categories provide a general overview of its classification.

Additionally, Paspalum langei has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
